description: A square designed slogan poster with a gritty, distressed black grunge-texture background resembling a chalkboard or scorched concrete wall. Running diagonally up the right side in large white chalk-style stenciled lettering is a stacked list: 'PEOPLE / MONEY / PRODUCTS / INTELLECTUAL / PROPERTY' — the categories ICE claims to police at the border. At upper-left, white sans-serif text reads 'If it crosses the U.S. border illegally, it's our job to', and the sentence is completed by a bold black-on-orange highlighted box reading 'STOP IT.' A rough orange/yellow painted cross or X mark is brushed over the lower-left of the chalk list, reinforcing the 'stop/prohibited' motif. The bottom-left bears the DHS/ICE seal with 'U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement' in white, and the bottom-right shows 'ICE.gov'. The aesthetic is deliberately tough and militaristic, using grunge texture and a stark color accent to project authority and deterrence. The accompanying tweet warns 'DO NOT enter the U.S. illegally.' Messaging technique: deterrence/mission-branding emphasizing ICE's broad enforcement scope. Target audience: would-be border crossers (deterrence) and the public/recruits.
notable_text: If it crosses the U.S. border illegally, it's our job to STOP IT. | PEOPLE MONEY PRODUCTS INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY | ICE.gov
